mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate from an extremely elevated level (>10%) signals the start of a labor market recovery. This reduces economic tail risk perception and increases forward earnings expectations, leading to positive equity returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE dropped from 14.7% in April to 13.3% in May 2020
failure_condition: The signal's rank information coefficient is negative or statistically insignificant during historical periods where UNRATE is above 10% and falling.

mechanism_narrative: A near-zero federal funds rate provides maximum monetary accommodation, lowering the discount rate for future corporate cash flows and supporting higher equity valuations. This effect provides a persistent tailwind as long as the rate remains at the effective lower bound.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.05% as of May 2020, indicating ZIRP
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not statistically higher than the long-term average during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month growth rate in retail sales exceeding 5% after a significant contraction signals a V-shaped recovery in consumer spending. This drives positive earnings surprises and boosts equity prices over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS collapsed in March/April 2020, setting up for a potential rebound
failure_condition: In months where RSAFS growth exceeds 5%, the average 20-day forward S&P 500 return is negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising industrial production and low, stable inflation indicates a healthy supply-side recovery without margin-compressing price pressures. This joint condition is bullish for equities as it supports both revenue growth and profitability.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 1-month change > 0 AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year change < 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO fell sharply, while CPI remains low
failure_condition: A portfolio that is long S&P 500 only when the trigger condition is met underperforms a buy-and-hold strategy over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The 3-month change in nonfarm payrolls is a direct proxy for the momentum of economic activity. Positive momentum indicates growing aggregate income and demand, which translates to higher corporate revenues and positive equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS showed a surprise increase of 2.5M in the May 2020 report
failure_condition: The correlation between the 3-month change in PAYEMS and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically positive (p > 0.05) over the full sample period.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1 million units on an annualized basis, it signals a severe contraction in residential investment, a key component of GDP. This weakness predicts lower future economic activity and negative returns for the broad market.
failure_condition: In periods following a HOUST reading below 1000, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a near-zero Fed Funds rate and a high unemployment rate (>8%) signals that conventional monetary policy has reached its limits and is failing to stimulate the real economy. This perception of policy impotence leads to negative investor sentiment and lower equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:As of June 2020, UNRATE is 13.3% and FEDFUNDS is 0.05%
failure_condition: The S&P 500 exhibits statistically significant positive returns in periods where both trigger conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: The quarter-over-quarter growth rate of real GDP serves as a backward-looking confirmation of economic health. A negative print confirms a contraction, leading to negative sentiment and short-term selling pressure on equities as the data is absorbed by the market.
evidence_refs: fact:Q1 2020 GDP showed a sharp contraction of -5.1%
failure_condition: The signal, defined as the sign of the most recent quarterly GDP growth, has a rank IC greater than -0.02 for 5-day forward returns over any 5-year rolling window.
